service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request repository inspection and bounded issue/pull request search using governed inputs such as owner, repo, query, state, labels, and limit.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Uses the governed service to inspect allowed repositories and search bounded issue and pull request context needed for day-to-day delivery work.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Read-only search and inspection should not require approval when the repository is allowlisted. Missing repository context should return clarification rather than broaden access.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can view bounded summaries and repository context only within explicitly allowed owner/repo scope. Should not receive raw repository export, secret material, or unbounded codebase access.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can prepare issue title, body, labels, assignees, and milestone as a preview. Can submit a request for issue creation through the governed flow if that execution step exists in the product design.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Prepares issue drafts with labels, assignees, and related metadata for later approved creation in an allowed repository.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Issue preparation is preview-only. Actual issue creation requires approval, but the approving actor is not identified in the brief.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see enough bounded repository and issue context to draft a relevant issue in an allowed repository, but not unrestricted repository contents or denied data classes.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can prepare a pull request comment using pull number, comment purpose, context, and optional visibility settings. Can request posting through the approval flow.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Prepares pull request comments from review or release context for governed posting to a pull request.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Comment preparation is preview-only. Posting the comment requires approval, but the approving role is not named in the brief.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can access bounded pull request context in allowed repositories sufficient to draft a comment, including the specific pull request identified by the request.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request workflow dispatch with workflow_id, ref, and bounded inputs for allowed repositories.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Requests GitHub Actions workflow runs within governed repository scope and mutation boundaries.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Workflow dispatch is approval-gated. Restricted workflows require an explicit grant in addition to normal approval, but the grant-holding actor is not identified in the brief.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see allowed workflow request surfaces for approved repositories and refs, but should not gain secret access, workflow bypass, or unrestricted backend control.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request draft release notes for a defined range and optional audience/include_prs settings.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Drafts release notes from bounded repository context without publishing a release.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Draft generation itself appears non-mutating and should not imply release publication authority. Publication is explicitly out of scope and requires approval if later introduced.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can view bounded issue, pull request, milestone, or comparison context needed to draft release notes within allowed repository scope.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can approve or reject governed mutation requests for issue creation, PR comment posting, and workflow dispatch according to repository scope and restriction rules.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Reviews governed mutation requests and associated records before issue creation, PR comment posting, or workflow dispatch can proceed.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Acts as the approval authority for gated mutations, but the exact business role and any differences by action type are not specified in the brief.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the prepared request, repository scope, approval records, and relevant audit metadata needed to make an approval decision within governed boundaries.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Users can complete the main governed GitHub tasks through business-safe capabilities for repository context search, issue preparation, pull request comment preparation, workflow dispatch request, and release-note drafting without exposing raw code-hosting operations.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows each named task is handled through a governed capability surface and does not require direct raw GitHub API or tool exposure to the agent.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Repository access stays explicitly bounded to allowed scope, and requests missing required repository, pull request, workflow, or range context stop with clear clarification-required behavior.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Reviewable examples show allowlisted repository scope is enforced and incomplete requests return explicit clarification-required outcomes instead of guessing or proceeding.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: All governed mutations remain previewed or approval-gated before execution, including issue creation, pull request comments, and workflow dispatches.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative flow review shows issue and comment actions produce previews first, workflow dispatches require approval, and no governed mutation executes without an approval step.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Governed outputs remain intentionally bounded, returning summaries, previews, requests, or drafts rather than raw repository export, secret access, workflow bypass, or unbounded codebase data.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Verification review confirms search returns bounded summaries, release notes remain draft-only, backend options stay bounded, and denied operations are blocked in representative cases.

success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Reviewers can inspect approval records and audit metadata for governed repository operations so they can understand what was requested, what was approved or denied, and within which repository scope.
success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM or verification review can point to audit and approval artifacts for representative read, preview, and mutation-request flows.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-oriented repository search and inspection can proceed within bounded scope, while issue creation, pull request comments, workflow dispatches, and release publication must stop at explicit approval boundaries. Drafting and preview are allowed where the brief marks them as preview-only or draft-only, but execution is not automatic.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product should answer only through bounded repository capabilities within explicit allowlisted scope. It should return summaries or drafts rather than raw repository export, ask for clarification when repository, pull request, workflow, or range context is missing, stop before executing issue creation, PR comment posting, workflow dispatch, or release publication without approval, and deny unsafe requests such as secret access, unbounded export, raw file exfiltration, or workflow bypass. Governed actions should remain auditable.

sections.description: How the system is decomposed into services and how those services are named and bounded.

linked_product_design_items.label: Repository Operator
linked_product_design_items.detail: Uses the governed service to inspect allowed repositories and search bounded issue and pull request context needed for day-to-day delivery work.

linked_product_design_items.label: Repository Operator visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can view bounded summaries and repository context only within explicitly allowed owner/repo scope. Should not receive raw repository export, secret material, or unbounded codebase access.

linked_product_design_items.label: Issue Requester
linked_product_design_items.detail: Prepares issue drafts with labels, assignees, and related metadata for later approved creation in an allowed repository.

linked_product_design_items.label: Issue Requester visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can see enough bounded repository and issue context to draft a relevant issue in an allowed repository, but not unrestricted repository contents or denied data classes.

linked_product_design_items.label: PR Comment Requester
linked_product_design_items.detail: Prepares pull request comments from review or release context for governed posting to a pull request.

linked_product_design_items.label: PR Comment Requester visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can access bounded pull request context in allowed repositories sufficient to draft a comment, including the specific pull request identified by the request.

linked_product_design_items.label: Workflow Requester
linked_product_design_items.detail: Requests GitHub Actions workflow runs within governed repository scope and mutation boundaries.

linked_product_design_items.label: Workflow Requester visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can see allowed workflow request surfaces for approved repositories and refs, but should not gain secret access, workflow bypass, or unrestricted backend control.

linked_product_design_items.label: Release Notes Drafter
linked_product_design_items.detail: Drafts release notes from bounded repository context without publishing a release.

linked_product_design_items.label: Release Notes Drafter visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can view bounded issue, pull request, milestone, or comparison context needed to draft release notes within allowed repository scope.

linked_product_design_items.label: Approval Reviewer
linked_product_design_items.detail: Reviews governed mutation requests and associated records before issue creation, PR comment posting, or workflow dispatch can proceed.

linked_product_design_items.label: Approval Reviewer visibility
linked_product_design_items.detail: Can inspect the prepared request, repository scope, approval records, and relevant audit metadata needed to make an approval decision within governed boundaries.

sections.label: Authority & Approval
sections.description: How restrictions, denials, approvals, and clarification posture become formal service behavior.

linked_product_design_items.label: Approval Posture Summary
linked_product_design_items.detail: Read-oriented repository search and inspection can proceed within bounded scope, while issue creation, pull request comments, workflow dispatches, and release publication must stop at explicit approval boundaries. Drafting and preview are allowed where the brief marks them as preview-only or draft-only, but execution is not automatic.

linked_product_design_items.label: Repository Operator approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Read-only search and inspection should not require approval when the repository is allowlisted. Missing repository context should return clarification rather than broaden access.

linked_product_design_items.label: Issue Requester approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Issue preparation is preview-only. Actual issue creation requires approval, but the approving actor is not identified in the brief.

linked_product_design_items.label: PR Comment Requester approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Comment preparation is preview-only. Posting the comment requires approval, but the approving role is not named in the brief.

linked_product_design_items.label: Workflow Requester approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Workflow dispatch is approval-gated. Restricted workflows require an explicit grant in addition to normal approval, but the grant-holding actor is not identified in the brief.

linked_product_design_items.label: Release Notes Drafter approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Draft generation itself appears non-mutating and should not imply release publication authority. Publication is explicitly out of scope and requires approval if later introduced.

linked_product_design_items.label: Approval Reviewer approval expectations
linked_product_design_items.detail: Acts as the approval authority for gated mutations, but the exact business role and any differences by action type are not specified in the brief.

linked_product_design_items.label: Repository Operator · Repository Context Access
linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow bounded issue and pull-request context search within an explicit allowlisted repository. Return summaries only, not raw repository export.

linked_product_design_items.label: Issue Requester · Issue Preparation and Creation Governance
linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Allow issue preparation preview with labels and assignees, but stop for approval before any issue is created downstream.

linked_product_design_items.label: Pr Comment Requester · Pull Request Comment Governance
linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Allow pull-request comment preparation preview from review or release context, but stop for approval before posting any comment.

linked_product_design_items.label: Workflow Requester · Workflow Dispatch Governance
linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Allow a workflow dispatch request to be prepared for an allowlisted repository and ref, but require approval before dispatch. Restricted workflows require explicit grant.

linked_product_design_items.label: Release Notes Drafter · Release Notes Drafting
linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow draft release notes from bounded repository context only. Do not create or publish a release.

linked_product_design_items.label: Repository Operator · Governed Repository Policy and Audit
linked_product_design_items.detail: Denied · Deny Request · Deny requests for secret exfiltration, raw file export, unbounded codebase dumps, or direct workflow bypass.

linked_product_design_items.label: Repository Operator · Repository Context Access
linked_product_design_items.detail: Restricted · Clarification Required · Stop and request clarification when repository context is missing or repository scope is not explicit.

linked_product_design_items.label: Pr Comment Requester · Pull Request Comment Governance
linked_product_design_items.detail: Restricted · Clarification Required · Stop and request clarification when pull request context is missing.

linked_product_design_items.detail: Restricted · Clarification Required · Stop and request clarification when workflow context is missing.

linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Behavior Summary
linked_product_design_items.detail: The product should answer only through bounded repository capabilities within explicit allowlisted scope. It should return summaries or drafts rather than raw repository export, ask for clarification when repository, pull request, workflow, or range context is missing, stop before executing issue creation, PR comment posting, workflow dispatch, or release publication without approval, and deny unsafe requests such as secret access, unbounded export, raw file exfiltration, or workflow bypass. Governed actions should remain auditable.

linked_product_design_items.label: Do not expose raw GitHub REST, GraphQL, or MCP tools directly to agents.
linked_product_design_items.detail: The product intent is a governed fronting service with ANIP-owned capability semantics, not broad code-hosting tool access.

linked_product_design_items.label: Do not allow unapproved execution of issue creation, pull request comment posting, workflow dispatch, or release publication.
linked_product_design_items.detail: The source requires explicit preview, approval, and mutation controls, and states these actions are not executed without approval.

linked_product_design_items.label: Do not support unbounded repository export, raw file export, codebase dumps, or secret access through repository capabilities.
linked_product_design_items.detail: The policy semantics explicitly deny secret exfiltration, raw file export, and unbounded repository export.

linked_product_design_items.label: Do not use backend options as a pass-through for arbitrary mutation payloads, workflow bypass, or other unsafe provider-specific behavior.
linked_product_design_items.detail: Backend options are only allowed for bounded provider features such as selected fields, pagination, or expand-like metadata.

linked_product_design_items.label: Do not operate outside explicit allowlisted repository scope or infer missing repository, pull request, workflow, or release range context.
linked_product_design_items.detail: The brief requires explicit repository scope and clarification-required behavior when key context is missing.

linked_product_design_items.label: Do not treat release-note drafting as release creation or publishing.
linked_product_design_items.detail: The defined capability is draft-only and explicitly does not create or publish a release.

sections.label: Audit & Lineage
sections.description: What evidence, lineage, and traceability the generated implementation must preserve.

linked_product_design_items.label: Users can complete the main governed GitHub tasks through business-safe capabilities for repository context search, issue preparation, pull request comment preparation, workflow dispatch request, and release-note drafting without exposing raw code-hosting operations.
linked_product_design_items.detail: Scenario review shows each named task is handled through a governed capability surface and does not require direct raw GitHub API or tool exposure to the agent.

linked_product_design_items.label: Repository access stays explicitly bounded to allowed scope, and requests missing required repository, pull request, workflow, or range context stop with clear clarification-required behavior.
linked_product_design_items.detail: Reviewable examples show allowlisted repository scope is enforced and incomplete requests return explicit clarification-required outcomes instead of guessing or proceeding.

linked_product_design_items.label: All governed mutations remain previewed or approval-gated before execution, including issue creation, pull request comments, and workflow dispatches.
linked_product_design_items.detail: Representative flow review shows issue and comment actions produce previews first, workflow dispatches require approval, and no governed mutation executes without an approval step.

linked_product_design_items.label: Governed outputs remain intentionally bounded, returning summaries, previews, requests, or drafts rather than raw repository export, secret access, workflow bypass, or unbounded codebase data.
linked_product_design_items.detail: Verification review confirms search returns bounded summaries, release notes remain draft-only, backend options stay bounded, and denied operations are blocked in representative cases.

linked_product_design_items.label: Reviewers can inspect approval records and audit metadata for governed repository operations so they can understand what was requested, what was approved or denied, and within which repository scope.
linked_product_design_items.detail: PM or verification review can point to audit and approval artifacts for representative read, preview, and mutation-request flows.

linked_product_design_items.label: Should this system keep a durable record of important actions?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Should those records be searchable later?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Should each execution be traceable as its own identifiable event?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Should related executions be grouped into one traceable unit of work?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Should the system preserve parent-child execution chains for later reconstruction?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes

linked_product_design_items.label: Should external systems be able to attach their own reference IDs?
linked_product_design_items.detail: Yes
